src_embedding_text: source: design test case id: tc-authentication-001 title: successful user registration with otp verification module: user registration source files or pages: 3 covered methods: preconditions: - user has a valid email and password. - server and database are operational. - network connectivity is stable. test data: - email: `testuser@example.com` - password: `securepass123!` - otp: `123456` (generated by server) test steps: 1. user opens the app and fills registration form with email and password. 2. app calls `doregistration()` on the server. 3. server saves user data to the database. 4. server generates otp (`123456`) and saves it to `password_reset_tokens` table. 5. user enters otp (`123456`) in the app. 6. app calls `sendotp()` with the otp. 7. server retrieves saved otp from the database and verifies it. 8. server generates and returns an authentication token to the app. expected result: - user account is created in the `users` table. - otp is stored in `password_reset_tokens` with an expiry date. - authentication token is returned to the app. - user is logged in automatically. unit: project:
